ddls:
CREATE TABLE "memory_mappings" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory mapping */,
  "addr_begin" TEXT /* Starting address of the memory mapping */,
  "addr_end" TEXT /* Ending address of the memory mapping */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the file associated with the memory mapping */,
  "is_executable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the memory mapping is executable */,
  "memory_mapping_type" TEXT /* Type of memory mapping (e.g., private, shared, anonymous) */,
  "page_table_id" INTEGER /* ID of the page table associated with the memory mapping */,
  "access_control_list_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access control list associated with the memory mapping */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_mappings_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES files ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_mappings_access_control_list_id FOREIGN KEY ("access_control_list_id") REFERENCES access_control_lists ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_mappings_page_table_id FOREIGN KEY ("page_table_id") REFERENCES page_tables ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "files" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., executable, data, configuration) */,
  "file_size" TEXT /* Size of the file in bytes */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the file was created */,
  "file_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the file (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the owner of the file */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_files_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processes" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each process */,
  "process_name" TEXT /* Name of the process */,
  "process_type" TEXT /* Type of the process (e.g., system, user) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the process was created */,
  "memory_mapping_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory mapping associated with the process */,
  "parent_process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent process */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the process (e.g., running, sleeping, zombie) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_memory_mapping_id FOREIGN KEY ("memory_mapping_id") REFERENCES memory_mappings ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_processes_parent_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, developer, end-user) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the user was created */,
  "access_control_list_id" INTEGER /* ID of the access control list associated with the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "page_tables" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each page table */,
  "page_table_name" TEXT /* Name of the page table */,
  "page_size" INTEGER /* Size of each page in bytes */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the page table was created */,
  "memory_mapping_id" INTEGER /* ID of the memory mapping associated with the page table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_control_lists" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control list */,
  "acl_name" TEXT /* Name of the access control list */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read, write, execute) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the access control list was created */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* ID of the file associated with the access control list */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the access control list */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_lists_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_control_lists_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES files ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interrupts" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interrupt */,
  "interrupt_type" TEXT /* Type of the interrupt (e.g., timer, I/O) */,
  "interrupt_number" INTEGER /* Number of the interrupt */,
  "trigger_date" TEXT /* Date the interrupt was triggered */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the interrupt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interrupts_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exceptions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exception */,
  "exception_type" TEXT /* Type of the exception (e.g., page fault, divide by zero) */,
  "exception_code" INTEGER /* Code of the exception */,
  "trigger_date" TEXT /* Date the exception was triggered */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the exception */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exceptions_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "error_type" TEXT /* Type of the error (e.g., memory error, file error) */,
  "error_code" INTEGER /* Code of the error */,
  "trigger_date" TEXT /* Date the error was triggered */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the performance metric was collected */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_usage" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory usage metric */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage value */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the memory usage was collected */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* ID of the process associated with the memory usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_usage_process_id FOREIGN KEY ("process_id") REFERENCES processes ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "file_system_metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file system metric */,
  "filesystem_name" TEXT /* Name of the file system */,
  "used_space" TEXT /* Used space on the file system */,
  "free_space" TEXT /* Free space on the file system */,
  "total_space" TEXT /* Total space on the file system */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date the file system metric was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
